title: Constellation Energy (CEG)Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Constellation Energy (CEG) compiled by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: AI data centers require 24/7 power, and nuclear is the only clean baseload option. CEG's existing fleet is irreplaceable. The 8% growth assumes margin expansion as old contracts expire and renew at higher PPA rates with hyperscalers.

verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

faq.question: Why use a two-stage DCF for Constellation Energy?
faq.answer: A two-stage DCF captures the immediate, accelerated cash flow growth driven by the current AI data center supercycle, followed by a normalized, lower-growth state that reflects the mature reality of running a utility over decades.

faq.question: Why might Operating Cash Flow be used instead of Free Cash Flow?
faq.answer: Utilities often carry heavy capital expenditure burdens (CapEx) to maintain aging infrastructure. In years where CapEx exceeds operating cash flow, Free Cash Flow goes negative. A DCF with a negative base FCF breaks down mathematically. Using Operating Cash Flow provides a proxy for the core business generation before major reinvestments.

faq.question: Is Constellation Energy a "tech stock"?
faq.answer: No, it is an unregulated power generator. However, because its carbon-free nuclear power is perfectly suited for AI data centers, it trades at a premium multiple compared to traditional utilities, making its stock performance highly correlated with tech infrastructure spending.
